_meta.description: 2026 US federal payroll/income-tax parameters + per-state income tax tables. Used by the paycheck calculator engine.
_meta.verification: All federal figures sourced Oct 2025 IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-32 (released for tax year 2026, filed 2027) and Tax Foundation. Verify each number against the cited source before launch. State tables below Texas are NOT yet populated — add per the state's Dept. of Revenue.

brackets._note: rate = marginal rate; upTo = upper bound of the band in annual taxable USD; null = infinity. Single band reused for Married-filing-separately for withholding purposes (matches W-4). VERIFY all thresholds against Rev. Proc. 2025-32.

fica._note: Employee-side rates. VERIFY wage base against SSA COLA release.

threshold._note: Liability threshold by filing status. Employer flat-withholds at 200000 regardless of status; this estimate uses status thresholds.

texas.name: Texas
texas._source: Texas has no state individual income tax (Texas Comptroller). https://comptroller.texas.gov/taxes/
texas.notes: No state income tax. Net pay = gross − federal withholding − FICA.

florida.name: Florida
florida._source: Florida levies no state individual income tax (Florida Dept. of Revenue). https://floridarevenue.com/

washington.name: Washington
washington._source: Washington has no tax on wage/salary income (WA Dept. of Revenue). A separate 7% capital-gains tax applies only to high investment gains, not wages. https://dor.wa.gov/
washington.notes: No state income tax on wages.

nevada.name: Nevada
nevada._source: Nevada levies no state individual income tax (Nevada Dept. of Taxation). https://tax.nv.gov/

tennessee.name: Tennessee
tennessee._source: Tennessee has no wage income tax; the Hall tax on interest/dividends was fully repealed effective 2021 (TN Dept. of Revenue). https://www.tn.gov/revenue

south-dakota.name: South Dakota
south-dakota._source: South Dakota levies no state individual income tax (SD Dept. of Revenue). https://dor.sd.gov/

wyoming.name: Wyoming
wyoming._source: Wyoming levies no state individual income tax (WY Dept. of Revenue). https://revenue.wyo.gov/

alaska.name: Alaska
alaska._source: Alaska levies no state individual income tax (Alaska Dept. of Revenue). https://tax.alaska.gov/

new-hampshire.name: New Hampshire
new-hampshire._source: New Hampshire has never taxed wage income; its Interest & Dividends tax was fully repealed effective 2025 (NH Dept. of Revenue Administration). https://www.revenue.nh.gov/
new-hampshire.notes: No state income tax on wages.

pennsylvania.name: Pennsylvania
pennsylvania._source: PA flat personal income tax 3.07% (unchanged since 2004), applied to compensation with no standard deduction or personal exemption. PA Dept. of Revenue. https://www.pa.gov/agencies/revenue/resources/tax-rates/personal-income-tax-rates
disclaimer: Separate local Earned Income Taxes (Act 32 EIT — e.g. ~3.79% Philadelphia) are levied by municipalities/school districts and are not included in this estimate.

illinois.name: Illinois
illinois._source: IL flat 4.95% (IL DOR Bulletin FY 2026-15). No standard deduction; personal exemption allowance $2,925 per allowance (IL-W-4). https://tax.illinois.gov/research/publications/bulletins/fy-2026-15.html
disclaimer: Estimate assumes 1 personal exemption allowance ($2,925) for single/head of household and 2 for married filing jointly. The exemption fully phases out above $250,000 (single) / $500,000 (MFJ) — not modeled.

north-carolina.name: North Carolina
north-carolina._source: NC flat 3.99% for tax years after 2025 (Session Law 2023-134); standard deduction $12,750 single / $25,500 MFJ / $19,125 HoH. NC Dept. of Revenue. https://www.ncdor.gov/taxes-forms/individual-income-tax/tax-rate-schedules
north-carolina.notes: Flat 3.99% after the NC standard deduction.

utah.name: Utah
utah._source: UT flat 4.45% for tax years beginning on/after Jan 1 2026 (SB60, signed Mar 23 2026). Base is federal taxable income. https://le.utah.gov/~2026/bills/static/SB0060.html
disclaimer: Utah's nonrefundable Taxpayer Tax Credit (which phases out with income) is not modeled, so this estimate may overstate tax for lower incomes. Base approximated as wages minus the federal standard deduction.

michigan.name: Michigan
michigan._source: MI flat 4.25%; 2026 personal exemption $5,900 per exemption (MI 2026 Withholding Guide, Form 446). https://www.michigan.gov/treasury
disclaimer: Estimate assumes 1 personal exemption ($5,900) for single/head of household and 2 for married filing jointly.
Several Michigan cities (Detroit, Grand Rapids, etc.) levy separate local income taxes not included here.

arizona.name: Arizona
arizona._source: AZ flat 2.5%; standard deduction $8,350 single / $16,700 MFJ & HoH (Tax Foundation 2026; AZ DOR inflation-adjusts annually). https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/state/state-income-tax-rates-2026/
disclaimer: Standard-deduction figures are the Tax Foundation 2026 values; confirm against the Arizona DOR final 2026 release before launch.
arizona.notes: Flat 2.5% after the AZ standard deduction.

colorado.name: Colorado
colorado._source: CO flat 4.40% applied to federal taxable income (after the federal standard deduction). Colorado has no separate state standard deduction. https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/state/state-income-tax-rates-2026/
disclaimer: Rate applies to federal taxable income; base approximated as wages minus the federal standard deduction. The rate can be temporarily reduced below 4.40% in TABOR-refund years — not modeled.

indiana.name: Indiana
indiana._source: IN state rate 2.95% for 2026 (DOR Departmental Notice #1, eff. Jan 1 2026). Personal exemption $1,000; no standard deduction. https://www.in.gov/dor/files/dn01.pdf
disclaimer: Estimate assumes a $1,000 personal exemption (single/HoH) / $2,000 (MFJ); dependent exemptions not modeled.
Indiana county income taxes apply on top of the state rate and vary by county — not included here.

kentucky.name: Kentucky
kentucky._source: KY flat 3.5% for tax year 2026 (reduced from 4.0%); standard deduction $3,360 (single statewide amount, all filing statuses). KY Dept. of Revenue. https://revenue.ky.gov/
disclaimer: Some Kentucky localities levy separate occupational/payroll taxes not included here.

mississippi.name: Mississippi
mississippi._source: MS 2026: 0% on first $10,000 of taxable income, 4.0% above (Build Up Mississippi Act). Standard deduction $2,300/$4,600/$3,400 plus exemption $6,000/$12,000/$9,500 (single/MFJ/HoH), combined here. MS DOR. https://www.dor.ms.gov/
mississippi.notes: 0% on the first $10,000 of taxable income, then 4.0%.

georgia.name: Georgia
georgia._source: GA flat 4.99% for 2026 (HB 463, signed May 2026, accelerated from 5.19%); standard deduction $15,000 single / $30,000 MFJ. GA Dept. of Revenue. https://dor.georgia.gov/taxes/important-tax-updates
georgia.notes: Flat 4.99% after the GA standard deduction.

louisiana.name: Louisiana
louisiana._source: LA flat 3.0% (Act 11, eff. 2025); 2026 combined standard deduction $12,875 single / $25,750 MFJ & HoH (LA DOR RIB 26-005, Jan 2026). https://revenue.louisiana.gov/
louisiana.notes: Flat 3.0% after the combined standard deduction.

iowa.name: Iowa
iowa._source: IA flat 3.8% for 2026 (Iowa DOR, SF 2442). Iowa conforms to the federal standard deduction. https://revenue.iowa.gov/press-release/2025-10-21/idr-announces-2026-individual-income-tax-and-interest-rates
disclaimer: Iowa's small personal-exemption tax credits ($40 single / $80 MFJ / $40 per dependent) are post-tax credits and are not modeled.

idaho.name: Idaho
idaho._source: ID flat 5.3% (HB 40, 2025) applied to taxable income above ~$4,811 single / $9,622 MFJ; conforms to the federal standard deduction. https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/state/state-income-tax-rates-2026/
idaho.notes: 5.3% on taxable income above the first ~$4,811 (single).

massachusetts.name: Massachusetts
massachusetts._source: MA flat 5% plus a 4% surtax (9% total) on taxable income above $1,107,750 (2026 indexed threshold). MA uses personal exemptions ($4,400 single / $8,800 MFJ / $6,800 HoH), not a standard deduction. https://www.mass.gov/info-details/massachusetts-4-surtax-on-taxable-income
disclaimer: Massachusetts' $1,000-per-dependent exemption is not modeled. The 4% surtax applies only to taxable income above $1,107,750.
